items.title: Anthropic releases Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic has launched its next-generation model family, Fable 5. As someone who relies on Claude Code to do the heavy lifting for your Next.js and Supabase stack, a flagship model upgrade directly translates to fewer hallucinations and better code generation. You can try this week by updating your Claude Code CLI or switching your system prompts to Fable 5 to see if it resolves any tricky state-management bugs in your AI-ingest project.

items.title: KnowledgeMCP: Turn any documentation into an MCP endpoint with zero LLM query costs
items.why_it_matters: To get better at directing AI, you need to feed it accurate context. This tool lets you turn any developer documentation into a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server without paying LLM token costs at query time, making it cheap and fast to keep your coding agent updated. You can try this week by running KnowledgeMCP locally with the latest Supabase or Next.js docs so Claude Code stops hallucinating outdated API methods.

items.title: Vercel AI Gateway Index: Claude dominates spend while DeepSeek takes token volume
items.why_it_matters: Vercel's production data shows that developers are heavily utilizing Claude for high-value tasks like coding, but routing high-volume, repetitive tasks to cheaper models. Since you are building AI-ingest on Vercel, this is a blueprint for keeping your own API costs low. You can try this week by setting up Vercel AI Gateway for your project and routing your heavy text-filtering tasks to a cheaper model while reserving Claude for the complex logic.

items.title: Agent-pd: A zero-token audit log to monitor Claude Code subagents
items.why_it_matters: When you let Claude Code run wild on your repository, it can sometimes make unexpected changes or run loops that waste your time. This lightweight tool creates a local, zero-token audit log of what your agent is doing. You can try this week by cloning the repo and running it alongside your next Claude Code session to safely monitor exactly which files are being modified in your AI-ingest codebase.
